Over the past few decades, public opinion in the UK has shifted significantly, particularly concerning end-of-life choices. There is a strong and growing support for "assisted dying" or "the right to die" in cases of terminal illness or unbearable suffering, which many see as a matter of individual autonomy and dignity. Therefore, UK public opinion tends not to fall at either extreme of "never" or "always."
